title: gonolobus plowmanii
intro: Gonolobus plowmanii, also known as Plowman's vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to Mexico and Guatemala. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Gonolobus plowmanii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for use in herbal medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
